summary: Validated mobile-portrait responsive fix for visual-mode-chips overlap with NOW TUNING header on solarisound dashboard. Logged in as admin and exercised five viewports (390x844, 360x800, 844x390, 768x1024, 1280x800). The user-reported bug (mobile portrait collision) is FIXED — zero 2D overlap between [data-testid='current-frequency'].parentElement and [data-testid='visual-mode-chips'] on all viewports <640px, with chips correctly relocating to bottom-left of the visualizer. Desktop/tablet/landscape preserve the top-right desktop layout. All 3 chips remain clickable on mobile and correctly toggle the gold accent active state. Play/pause button is visible/clickable across all viewports and successfully toggles window.__audioEngine.playing. NOTE: at 1280x800 desktop the chips top-right still numerically overlap (ho=70.8, vo=17) with the centered NOW TUNING header because the visualizer column is only 504px wide between the two side columns — but this is the ORIGINAL pre-fix behavior (the new className uses `sm:top-4 sm:right-4` which is byte-identical to the legacy `top-4 right-4`). Not a regression of this fix; reported separately as a pre-existing minor issue.

issues: PRE-EXISTING (not introduced by this iter's fix): at 1280px desktop the visualizer center column is ~504px wide; the centered 'NOW TUNING / 432.0 Hz' header (159px) and the top-right chips (226px) overlap by ho=70.8px / vo=17px. The 'RINGS' chip visibly covers the right side of the 'NOW TUNING' label. Mobile portrait fix works; this desktop case is a separate visual collision the iter-30 fix did not address. Mitigation idea: move chips below the header on narrow desktop columns (e.g. add `top-14` on lg+ or move into the transport row).

action_items: Optional follow-up: address the desktop NOW-TUNING ↔ chips overlap at 1280x800 (pre-existing, screenshot shows 'NOW TUN' partially covered by RINGS chip). The current fix does NOT regress this — it merely preserves the legacy desktop position via `sm:top-4 sm:right-4` — but the same collision pattern that triggered the mobile user report is also visible on narrow desktop columns.
critical_code_review_comments: Dashboard.jsx is 1350 lines — exceeds the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting visualizer/transport/sidebar sections into sub-components for maintainability.
The visual-mode chips className uses arbitrary hex values inside Tailwind brackets (border-[#C4A67A]/60). Working correctly via Tailwind JIT but consider extracting to theme tokens for consistency.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on the targeted mobile-portrait acceptance criterion (5/5 viewport overlap checks where the bug applied passed; 1 pre-existing desktop overlap flagged separately)

context_for_next_testing_agent: iter-30 was a pure CSS responsive fix for the visual-mode-chips container in Dashboard.jsx (~line 947) — `absolute z-10 flex gap-1.5 bottom-4 left-3 sm:bottom-auto sm:left-auto sm:top-4 sm:right-4`. Fix verified on portrait <640px (chips at bottom-left of visualizer, zero 2D overlap with NOW TUNING header). Important quirk for future tests: the dashboard stacks vertically on <lg (1024px) viewports so the visualizer center column appears far down the page (top≈2200px on iPhone). When clicking chips on mobile, scroll element into view first (scroll_into_view_if_needed) — direct page.click() may miss because the element is below the initial fold. Login path: landing page → click [data-testid='landing-start-button'] → fill [data-testid='auth-email-input'] + [data-testid='auth-password-input'] → click [data-testid='auth-submit-button'].

desktop_1280x800.note: PRE-EXISTING overlap, not introduced by this fix

interaction_tests.chip_toggle_desktop: PASS — chladni, ripples, rings each become active (gold border rgba(196,166,122,0.6)) after click
interaction_tests.play_pause_toggle_mobile: PASS — window.__audioEngine.playing toggled false → true on click

rca of the issue: Root cause of original bug: visual-mode-chips container used `absolute top-4 right-4` placing it in the top-right of the visualizer panel. On narrow viewports (<640px) the centered NOW TUNING header (text-4xl, ~160px wide at the column center) and the right-aligned chips row (~225px wide) competed for the same horizontal band at the top of the panel and collided. Fix: added Tailwind responsive prefixes so the chips drop to `bottom-4 left-3` on <640px and only return to `top-4 right-4` on `sm+`. Verified by measuring getBoundingClientRect overlap = 0 on all <640px viewports. Reproduction: open dashboard on iPhone 12 portrait emulation (390x844), inspect the top of visualizer panel — pre-fix the RINGS chip overlapped 'NOW TUN' text; post-fix the chips sit at the lower-left and the header is clear.
